entry.title: MFS Testing strategy for smooth transition and reliable performance
entry.content:
It is crucial to test the MFS solution thoroughly before Go Live to ensure a smooth transition and reliable performance. Different projects require different test strategies. Below, I share some general approaches I followed in various MFS implementation projects.

During the build phase, we usually use an ABAP program (SCWM/MFS_TEST_MFS_RECEIVE2 or a custom program) to test our solution. However, this does not provide visibility into how the PLC would react to different situations. Therefore, we use emulation software where the warehouse automation layout is modelled and all possible logic is implemented. In SIT, we typically use this emulation software to test the MFS solution.

Many cases, existing PLC logic is changed to ensure it can work with MFS solution. Even sometimes a master PLC is introduced which communicate with lower-level PLC or hardware’s & then communicate with MFS with telegram messages. So, it is important to test these PLC changes. For this we usually place the new version of PLC software in place of the existing Legacy PLC & do the test with MFS. These kind of test needs warehouse down time.

Many warehouses run 24/7 & getting a down time is a big challenge. So, it is very crucial to plan these kinds of tests in detail so we can utilize this downtime to the fullest. This is also a great opportunity to test all your warehouse automation & integration with other software’s.

We usually try to replicate a day after Go Live in the testing environment which gives us a very good visibility & confidence how the EWM/MFS solution would work in production environment.

 

 Operational test Planning:

We need to ensure following key items are addressed before we start operational testing for Warehouse Automation with MFS.

Master data: Need to ensure all the master data such as products, packaging specifications, any other data entries are in place in test system so you can use these production data for these tests. These test data also need to be available in other software test system. You may need to block bins not part of the agreed test scope so to ensure testing process does not mess up current legacy production inventory.

Network issues: Network team need to set up the firewall rules so Test MFS system can communicate with Test PLC without any issues. Similarly, connection between Test EWM/MFS system, Test Fleet management software which controls the AGV & any other application which we would like to test as part of end to end operational test needs to be in place.

Resources:It is crucial to have resources from business teams and all other applications available during planning & execution of operational test. All warehouse key resources should be trained on the new MFS processes so they can participate actively and appreciate the test results..

Test Scenarios: You need to have test cases covering both happy paths and all exception scenarios. Decide how to recreate exception scenarios. For example, to simulate a "No Read" scenario from a scanner, you might need to cover the scanners so the PLC sends telegrams accordingly.

Test Cases/Pallets: Ensure you have enough cases and pallets with all possible data variations. For partial pallet scenarios, have partial pallets ready. Also, include oversize pallets, mixed pallets, etc. Decide the entry and exit points of these pallets, Cases for each test. For instance, in the Goods Receipt process, determine where pallets should enter and where they should be stored. If cases are used to build pallets, decide where cases need to enter the system and where pallets should come out.

Rollback Approach: It is critical to have a solid roll back plan so after the test everything is brought back to BAU status. 

Operational Test Execution:

During the test, all the test results, observations need to recorded correctly. Not everything goes as planned, so need to be able to change the approach on the fly with due consideration to overall design architecture.  

After Test execution:

During the test, things move very fast & so many transaction data gets posted. So after the completion of the test, it is important to analyse the data to see of anything has any abnormal posting.Log all defects & track for its closure.

It is very critical to complete the rollback steps before handing over to business. Such as replacing the test PLC with current production PLC, changing the IP address at MFS. Network team reverting the Firewall rules. If any test EWM labels are used, it is necessary to replace with actual labels. If any BAU labels are damaged, ensure to reprint them in current legacy software. Do a stock count & ensure they are in right places. Sometimes you need to bring back the pallets, cases to its original positions or manually adjust the new position of these stock in legacy WMS application.

I hope this blog provides valuable insights into conducting a comprehensive MFS test. I would love to hear your feedback and learn about your experiences in implementing such tests in your warehouse.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Time Off Implementation
entry.content:
Recently we went thru a Time Off Implementation. Time Off is a core area and has various tentacles into including, but not limited to Payroll and S/4 ( for CATS based costings Time Sheets, Work Order scheduling in PM etc). I will be demonstrating the configuration of Time Off and the subsequent configuration of interfacing with EC Payroll and S/4. In addition to that I will be demonstrating the calculation of provisions in Payroll (Sometimes referred to as leave provisioning) and will be ending this with a demonstration of Purchase Leave and its interfacing with payroll. This will be a long series and while readers will find this attuned to Australia (and it is), the concepts can be used in other jurisdictions as well. 

The series will be divided into five parts:

1. Time Off solution with a demonstration of Entitled-as-Accrued Leave like Annual Leave and its integration/replication with  EC Payroll along with the necessary settings for provisioning calculations.

2. An addition of another specific type of leave, which has some calendar based entitlements. Meaning, while the leave accrues at its own clip but the user can only avail it after a certain time frame. Do the time and get the claim. Once again, we will revisit the replication into EC Payroll and provisioning calculations. I will be demonstrating the Long Service Leave along with some flavours.

3. Addition of other leave types which does not attract any provisions. Use-it-or-lose it types( a poor characterisation but...). I will be demonstrating Sick Leave. Additional non account based leaves (think of Wellness Leave, Volunteering and Mental Health leaves etc ) will be covered.

4. Purchase Leave set up, wherein you can take a purchase leave from the organisation for a salary deduction over the period of span of PL. 

5. Additional aspects, like integrating Time Off with S/4 and using multiple Time Off Types on a same quota etc and notes around various Time Off related runs, RBP permission set up etc. 

Intuitive users, particularly old timers from SAP HR will recollect that:
1. Time Off Type => Absence Types ( or Attendance types, but I will limit it to Absence type for our purposes, AWART).

2. Time Account Type => Quota Type (KTART)

3. Time Account => Amount of Time Account Type, essentially the quota amount. 

This is the first part of the series and as explained earlier, it will cover an entitled-as-accrued kind of leave. Think of Annual Leave where you are entitled to a "certain" amount of leave for each day. This part will explain the set up. 

Think of an employment contract like this:

Employment ContractAmount of Annual Leave for 1.0 FTE
Contract A150 Hours
Contract B180 Hours

I have mentioned a simple case of contract but you could think of expanding the table with additional attributes like locations and different entitlements as per locations etc. In fact the config details show a more practical set up then a theoretical one.

Some explanation of elements:
1. Time Profile: Think of this as a config element and they are really an instance of the MDF object and you can create them using Manage Data or Manage Time Off Structures. These master data element is assigned to an employee in their job info and they basically are more or less a list of absences (not just absences but for our conversation, it suffices to think of them as such) that an employee is allowed to attempt to apply. I say "attempt" because the actual application still depends on things like quota(SF - Time Account balances).

The Time Profile element.

In the below config, this TP shows that an employee has access to the listed Time Off types ( Annual, Sick, LSL, Purchase) 

Example of an employee's Job Info record which shows the assignment of the Time Profile to the employee

2. Time Type. Sometimes referred to as Time Off type or even Absence Type by old time SAP HR folks, they represent the actual Time Off type. As a user you will apply for a leave under this code.  The various attributes are either self explanatory or easily understood via the in line help icon. Of essence, is the Time Account Posting Rules, which denotes which Time Account Type to deduct from. In the part 2 of the series, I will introduce the counting rules etc but for this case, this is an extremely simple case of requesting a leave that can be either be requested for the full day or requested in fraction of hours but in full hours. Meaning, you can request a full day of AL worth 7.5 Hours or 1,2,3,4,5,6 or 7 hours but not really 1 hours 15 minutes. Time Type also allows you to specify the WF that you would trigger. For example, a simple WF to line manager would suffice for AL but something like Paid Parental Leave may require a WF that goes to the payroll/HR team after being approved by the line manager. Horses 4 Courses. 

The most important bit is the Time Account Posting Rules. In this case, as the Time Type screenshot shows, it "posts" to Time Account Type 10. In simple terms it means deduct it from Time Account of type 10. TAT can be thought of quota and we will visit the configuration, in detail, for this element in due course. Of course, in real life you will start with configuring the Time Account Type first and then Time Off type before including them in Time Profile. Once again there are many characteristics and many of them are self explanatory but using the help icon really helps. I will explain the annotated elements, though. 

1. External Code - Think of your KTART but really an external code identifying the Account. 

2. Entitlement Method - I encourage reading the possible options and we will be visiting them in later series but in this case, it is marked as Entitled and Accrued. This means, when the Time Evaluation runs for the employee ( there is no such term in SF, the correct term is Accrual Run ) it calculates the accrual for that day and any past days that may be pertinent due to master data changes and simply transfers the calculated Accrual as entitlement. So if your balance is 50 hours at the end of 30 June and if you check your balance at the end of 1 July, the balance would be bumped up with the accrual for that day. 

3. Accruable Rule Data Effective Date: What this really means is, whats the date the Job Info of the employee is read as and when the various Rules are running. In case of Daily accrual run for a TAT which has Accrual Frequency Period as daily, this doesn't really matter as start date = end date. 

4. Account Creation Type: This really defines the periodicity of the Time Account. Permanent Accounts are forever. You have other options there Recurring and Adhoc. If you have use it or lose it withing the time frame kind of Time Account, then that would be recurring. Ad hocs are non regular types, we will explore them in part 5.

5. Level of Accrual Automation - You have options of Manual and Automatic. Automatic really means, the Accrual runs are system generated and that you do not have to schedule it. Manual means you have to set up a Accrual Run job using Manage Time Off Calendars to do that. In our case, I have set it up as Automatic and you can see it here (Administer Time under People Profile/Time Off Info):
    

 

6. Recruit Rule. In this rule you will code what happens when an employee is recruited/hired. In this case, all we do is create an account and credit 0 hours. This is like opening a bank account with $0 deposit.

7. Accrual rule: This is the rule which calculates the Accrual as and when the accrual run happens. 

What this rule does is calculate the number of hours. Trace back to the original snippet of requirements.

Real life requirements will be lot more complex. So what we do is create a look up table and use it for basing our calculations. Think of look up table as T559E table of SAP HR, only with the added advantage of being able to add additional fields.  A single entry from the lookup table:

What this really means, the employee in the mentioned personnel area ( XX ) with the Contract XY 150 Hours has 150 hours of Annual Leave entitlement. 

This is the actual look up table itself. Notice how different contract has different number of hours of Annual Leave. The contract itself would be a field that is maintained against the employee' job info. 

 

Notice that the Accrual rule reads the base entitlement and gets a per day equivalent ( which again differs based on Leap and Non leap year) and multiplies it by the FTE to arrive at a per day accrual calculation. If you go back to point 5, this shows up as 15 minutes. Which is really, (150/365)*0.6(FTE) = 0.246575 which is 15 minutes. In Series 5, I will show how you can stop accrual under some conditions (for example, if the employee is on Leave Without Pay or on Industrial Action Leave etc). 

8. Level of Simulation Precision: If you are applying for a future date, let's say 1 Jan 2025 to 30 Jan 2025 with today being 1 June 2024, then this simulates the Time Account accrual rule up to the end date of the leave application based on either exact or approximate simulation. Think of this as quota projection. Complex accrual rules would take a few seconds and simple projections take a fraction of the time. I have not really tried with simple projections but I suppose it will look for just any job info changes as opposed to running it for every single day. 

Now, lets look how the calculations look like. You can use the Time Account Process Simulator to check this and see how it calculates the accruals. 

The above part explain the TO configurations. Now to the payroll part. Normally, payroll would have the following requirements. This is not exhaustive but would suffice.

1. Any Leave Taken must be replicated to EC Payroll so as to be able to ascribe a $ value. This is particularly important in Australia as the ATO expects any payments made to the employee for paid leaves ( AL, LSL, SL etc ) are reported via STP.

2. Accruals are transferred to EC Payroll so as to be able to calculate the provisions. Provisions are amounts that the company must hold as liabilities in their balance sheet and have enough cash/liquid assets to be able to pay the employee in case they are terminated. Now you know, why companies insist on employees taking their full quota of leaves. It reduces their liabilities and provisions. 

Lets tackle the second point first and then the first point. Provisions are calculated traditionally in on prem SAP PY system incorporating an integrated TM and PY module using the function QLVPR, which is used in subschema QLVP:

 

In the payroll log, you would be looking for:

What the system does is, it calculates the provisions amount based on various config maintained in the EC Payroll system. In essence, you would be maintaining interest rates, escalation rates and probability. The calculation is based on the entitlement hours and hourly rate with some escalations, probability and interest rates built in. In traditional SAP HR on prem system the accrual and entitlement hours are supplied by the B2 cluster table. But in EC Payroll there are no time cluster tables really (they are there and can be used as well but that's a topic for another day). EC Payroll instead expects the hours to be interfaced ( replicated) to Time Infotype 2010. The calculations would take place on the basis of the hours supplied into IT2010. 

Lets look at the configuration on how to achieve this. 

To replicate the accrual hours into payroll, you need to create a snapshot of time data. A snapshot is frozen in time, as of a specific date, entitlement and accrual hours. So technically, if you are running the pay on 15th of the month for the monthly pay, you will "project" and "take a snapshot" of Time Account balances as of the last day of the month (28/29, 30/31 whatever the lucky month). 

This is what the snapshot for an employee looks like and a snapshot is created for every single employee:

You create a snapshot using Time Off Calendar:

Typically, the start date would be the start date of the month but do take it right up to end of the month. Notice how the snapshot is created per Time Account Type. Provisioning are required for a month (as FI accounting periods are monthly) and hence the calculations must project to the end of the month. In Series 5, I will show how this can be automated.

A snapshot can be verified under manage data:

 

To replicate this into EC Payroll, you will need a Data Replication configuration object:

On running the snapshot this will create a Data Replication Proxy, if a replication configuration exists:

 

 

Again one proxy per user is created whose snapshot has been created. A status of Sync Confirmed means it has been replicated into ECP and Out of sync means it is yet to be replicated. 

 

Once again, to replicate the snapshot into IT2010 you will need a wagetype and that wage type has to be mapped to the Time Account Type external code. These WTs will have to be permissioned in T512Z appropriately to be entered in 2010.

This mapping is carried out using table - T77SFEC_CVMAPC

Check the screenshot of IT2010 along with the mapping entry. See how are how the entitlement hours are being received into WT 9578. There is no Accrual hours ( as the leave type is entitled as accrued) but when we take a look at LSL we will see Accrual hours in action as well.

In the view V_HRSFEC_T5QLM we map back the wagetypes to quota types. This also tell us that Time Account Type external codes can be different to Quota types as long as we get the mapping and reverse mapping right. 

The actual replication between ECP and EC is carried out using transaction HRSFEC_PTP_ABSE_REPL. While testing this, it does not hurt to inspect the JSON

The output, if you chose, JSON would be:

And the IT2010 would look like this. The hours are multiplied by 1000. 

Lets cast our eyes back to the Data Replication object. Notice how the object changes to Sync confirmed:

Run the pay and notice the following:

We replicated for the month of June and the new calcs are based on May + snapshot hours for June.

There is a second part to this as well. The actual replication of the leave into IT2001 and any valuation thereof. If the external code of time type is different to the actual absence type in ECP then you will have to maintain this in T77SFEC_CVMAPC but if its same, then it is not really required. Running the absence replication would get you the entry into PA2001. 

An approved absence in EC

Corresponding entry in ECP

In case you are interested in linking tables, this would be it.

The key on this table would match the key on employee time object

The RAB function in Payroll schema would do the rest for you.

You can valuate the way you want it. As an example with a valuation basis, we remunerate the same and manifest that in payslip. In addition to that report it to ATO via the proper evaluation class configuration.

 

Hope this helps. In part 2, we will see the configuration of LSL and introduce the concept of deductions from Time Accounts based on some calculations. LSL will also throw up accruals as opposed to just entitlements due to the nature of the account type. 

Happy Time off times!

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano, 

I am so excited about the SAP Learning digital skills initiative and have always found the ERP interesting. As a recent college graduate facing challenges in securing employment amidst the global economic downturn, this opportunity would be an amazing way to start me on a new career as an SAP consultant.
Thank you so much for this opportunity!

entry.title: Re: JobService en SAP B1 última versión
entry.content:
It's very simple, @nidhisingh14 you just have to perform a new installation of that patch and you can check it yourself.


The workflow service in Windows is and running, but from the sld you cannot access said service, http://404 not found

I am in an installation migration process and I have decided not to go to FP2405 because of this error with no apparent solution and without typification and to stay on FP2402 to my displeasure.

entry.title: API Anomaly Detection in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s interconnected digital world, where data flows across various systems, ensuring the integrity and security of APIs has become paramount. API Anomaly Detection plays a crucial role in safeguarding these critical communication channels.

API Anomaly Detection refers to the process of identifying unusual or unexpected behaviour within API traffic. It involves continuous monitoring of API requests and responses, analysing patterns, and detecting deviations from established norms. By doing so, organizations can swiftly identify potential security threats, performance bottlenecks, or operational irregularities.

Why is API Anomaly Detection Important?

  • Performance Optimization: Anomalies in API traffic can impact performance. By detecting irregularities early, organizations can optimize API usage, reduce latency, and enhance overall system efficiency.
  • Business Continuity: APIs are the backbone of modern applications, supporting seamless interactions between services. Anomalies can disrupt critical processes, affecting user experience and business continuity.
  • Security: APIs are gateways to sensitive data and services. Any abnormal activity could signal a cyberattack, data breach, or unauthorized access. Effective anomaly detection helps prevent security breaches and ensures data confidentiality.

Below are some real-world scenarios where API anomalies can have significant consequences. Having a system in place that identifies these anomalies can prove extremely beneficial to organizations and their business:

Flash Sale Traffic Surge:

    • Scenario: During a flash sale, a financial institution’s payment processing API experiences sudden high traffic.
    • Issue: Without proper anomaly detection, delays in transaction processing may occur, affecting user experience and revenue.
    • Solution: Anomaly detection identifies the traffic surge, allowing efficient load handling.

Flight Booking Availability Drops:

    • Scenario: An airline’s flight booking API encounters availability drops.
    • Issue: Unavailability can lead to frustrated customers and lost bookings.
    • Solution: Anomaly detection pinpoints the cause (e.g., server failure, network issues), enabling prompt resolution.

Unusually Large Transactions in an Online Marketplace:

    • Scenario: An online marketplace’s payment API detects unusually large transactions from a single account.
    • Issue: Such transactions may indicate fraud or money laundering.
    • Solution: Anomaly alerts trigger further investigation, safeguarding financial integrity.

Suspicious Login Patterns in E-Commerce Authentication:

    • Scenario: An e-commerce platform’s authentication API faces multiple failed login attempts from different IP addresses.
    • Issue: Brute-force attacks threaten user accounts and system security.
    • Solution: Anomaly detection identifies suspicious patterns, thwarting unauthorized access.

 

SAP API Management & Anomaly Detection

SAP Integration Suite is an industry-leading and enterprise-grade integration platform-as-a-service that helps businesses seamlessly connect and integrate their applications, data, and processes within their organization and beyond. APIs are the building blocks for many such integrations. The API Management capability in SAP Integration Suite is a complete solution, that addresses all enterprise requirements for API security and governance. It offers advanced analytical capabilities to track API usage and performance. In order to strengthen the API Management capability and empower organizations to further safeguard their digital highways, we introduce the API Anomaly Detection feature as part of the latest release of SAP Integration Suite.  

This is one of the many exciting AI features from BTP that was announced by Mr. Juergen Mueller, CTO in the SAP Sapphire Innovation Keynote! You can find the recording of the entire keynote presentation and demo here.

Note : The feature is in the process of getting updated in our global Data centres. Check this Note for information about regional availability.

Note : The availability of the Anomaly Detection feature is dependent on your SAP Integration Suite service plan. Check this Note for information about the various plans and supported features.

 

Feature overview

API Owners create and deploy APIs using the API Management tools available in the Integration Suite. Their critical task is to ensure that the deployed APIs remain consistently available and deliver optimal performance, thereby ensuring business continuity and satisfying customers. To assist API Owners in fulfilling their responsibilities, we introduce the Machine Learning based API Anomaly Detection feature. It entails educating an AI model to learn the behaviour of managed APIs (API proxies) deployed on a tenant.  It works by comparing a current timeframe of API calls with the corresponding past time-series data.

Enablement

As an Administrator of a specific Integration Suite - API Management tenant, one could decide to turn the feature on from the Settings -> APIs page of the Integration Suite application with a simple toggle switch. As there is a model training involved everytime this configuration is altered, frequent changes to this configuration are not recommended.

A key aspect of the feature is also the ability to send alert notifications on detection of any abnormalities. For this, the BTP Alert Notification Service is utilized. With minimal configurations, the preferred notification channel such as email, SMS etc. could be established that shall then be used to send the alerts in the event of an anomaly.

That’s it! The AI model is set into motion, and it is constantly watching the API traffic flowing through this tenant for any deviations from the normal established patterns. When it encounters such an unusual event, it immediately sends an alert on the preferred channel to the configured contacts so that appropriate actions could be taken.

There is also a possibility that optionally, certain critical APIs could be chosen to be specifically monitored for anomalies. This ensures that the abnormalities in the selected APIs are also flagged explicitly even if the overall system/tenant traffic seems to be behaving as usual.

Anomaly details

The Analytics dashboard is enriched with the Anomaly data as & when they occur. The most recent anomalies are visible upfront, but the historical anomalies could also be found on selection of past dates.

As of today, the AI model is capable of flagging anomalies around API Call count (increase or decrease in call count), API Error count (increase in number of server and client errors) & API Performance (increase or decrease in end-to-end response time).  

Each of the anomalies detected has further information such as how much is the deviation from the normal, when did the anomaly occur, which of the APIs or backends were involved, which developers experienced the anomaly etc. All of this is crucial information for the API Owner to understand the root cause of the situation and the impact of the unusual event. This might help them decide on the next set of actions to be undertaken to bring the situation back to normalcy and also to avoid such occurrences in the future.

Apart from the anomaly insights, there are also a set of recommended actions suggested automatically by the tooling that could provide a direction to the API Owners on the next course of remedial action.

Anomaly evaluation

There could be some situations where the AI model recognizes something as unusual but the API Owners could find an explanation to call it a normal occurrence. In such circumstances, the API Owners could mark the event as not an anomaly and let the AI model learn from it for future evaluations.

 

Summary

API Anomaly Detection involves monitoring and identifying abnormalities in time series data related to APIs. It helps API owners detect unexpected patterns or deviations from the norm, ensuring optimal performance and business continuity. SAP Integration Suite offers API Anomaly Detection as part of the API Management capability. The initial release identifies anomalies around API call count, error & performance in a tenant. More information could be found in our help documentation here.

As we continue to enhance and refine our offering, we eagerly await your feedback on this initial feature. Please try it out and share your thoughts with us!

entry.title: Information about SAP BODS and Salesforce API Compatibility
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently working on a project for a client who uses ETL SAP Business Object Data Services 4.2 SP14. In this version, we are attempting to import data from Salesforce using the standard Salesforce.com adapter. However, we have discovered that this adapter only supports Salesforce APIs up to version 21.0.

I would like to inquire if upgrading to the latest version, SAP BODS 4.3 SP0, would enable us to use APIs beyond version 30.0. This is crucial for us as the older API versions will be deprecated in Salesforce's '25 release, and they do not allow us to import certain Salesforce objects and fields essential for our project.

Thank you for your assistance. Please let me know if you need any further information.

Best regards,
Edoardo

#SAP BODS 4.2 SP14 #SAP BODS 4.3 SP0

entry.title: Re: Tips to add your SAP Build Apps application to different BTP environments - Part 4
entry.content:
Hi Maharana,

To experience SSO login, try to open in an incognito window.

Hope this helps!

Best regards,
Aparna

entry.title: Design and Discover title in SAP Integration suite is not showing copy and create
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am just trying to get familiar myself  with SAP integration suite. I have assigned all the necessary roles to my user id for designing and developing integration flow. Also have assigned all the capabilities for Cloud Integration and so on 

Screenshot for reference:

 

But I dont see Copy and Create in Discover and Design title of Integration suite.

Discover no copy 

Design no create

Can someone help me as to how can i get the Copy and create enabled.

#SAPIntegartionsuite

entry.title: Re: How to handle Purchase Orders and Sales Orders with Old Material Numbers?
entry.content: Hope you tried the option of Material substitution(VB11/12/13) for such scenarios?

entry.title: Re: Inventory Management Output determination
entry.content: Check the Output procedure assigned to it or not.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Call Monitor (SCMON) – Analyze usage of your code
entry.content:
Hi @taryckbensaili ,

usage data collected by SCMON must be aggregated with the SUSG (see in the beginning of the blog), SUSG snapshots can be transported (download to file/upload from file) from the production to development system, from where they can be used by Custom Code Migration app (see "Add usage data..." chapter in the blog).

Kind regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: How to handle Purchase Orders and Sales Orders with Old Material Numbers?
entry.content: also for partially open & return orders-how your client wants accordingly this can be designed(like a zero qty old material came in after 1month-what is expectation of client-hope you already explored MB1b with 309 movement type options(just sharing the thoughts)

entry.title: Re: Tips to add your SAP Build Apps application to different BTP environments - Part 4
entry.content: Hi Aparna, I have tried in incognito mode also but i'm not getting the sign in screen. App is directly opening.

entry.title: Re: Use SAP Document Management service (DMS), Integration Option to Store Document Files
entry.content:
Hello @TiaXu ,

Good day!

I have tried to implement the same approach mentioned above for DMS integration in BTP, but facing some errors while running the application in cloud foundry.

can you please help me.

 

 

Regards,

Ganesh.

entry.title: How to remove sub-report data links from the sdk
entry.content:
I use the crystal report SDK to generate pdf files. We're currently trying to use dataSet as a data source. Everything works but the subReports are empty after export. We modified the rpt: -> database expert -> links -> remove link and save the subReport and it works. The idea would be to reproduce the same thing from the SDK. I.e. go from a query with “INNER JOIN” to just “SELECT...”.

Acutal : 

`AdditionalFees__`.`AdditionalFeeDescription__`
FROM `AdditionalFees__` `AdditionalFees__` INNER JOIN `GdrFields` `GdrFields` ON `AdditionalFees__`.`parentKey`=`GdrFields`.`key`

Want this 
SELECT `language`.`TaxSummaryNetAmount`, `language`.`TaxSummaryVAT`, `language`.`TaxSummaryTaxAmount`
FROM `language` `language`

entry.title: Re: Document Management Service, Integration Option
entry.content: hello is this post visible? my questions keep getting deleted for some reason ...

entry.title: How to change International Ship to Address for ShipTo Partner in Sales Order
entry.content:
Hello 

I have a requirement to change the international Ship To address in Sales Order whenever its National Address is changed.

Currently via standard mechanism , only the POSTAL code and COUNTRY is being copied.

Can anyone suggest a BADI or a USER EXIT to do the required change? 

In short , my requirement is to copy any changes from Screen 1(national address) to Screen 2(International Address)

Currently as per standard logic only Country and Postal Code are being copied.

Steps to reach here :

Open any Sales Order

-> Display Header Details

->Partner Tab

-> Double click on the ShipTo Partner number , The popup that opens up is National Address

on the top of the pop up screen you might find a button 'International Versions' , this should have your international address. 

 

Regards,

Shruti Poojary

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
It seems you tried to log on with the user DevChallenger and incorrect password too many times. You can see the policy:

~/sap/hdbclient/hdbsql -U myDBAdmin -jA "SELECT * FROM M_PASSWORD_POLICY"

...which by default is:

| PROPERTY                                    | VALUE |
| ------------------------------------------- | ----- |
| force_first_password_change                 | true  |
| last_used_passwords                         | 5     |
| minimum_password_lifetime                   | 1     |
| maximum_password_lifetime                   | 182   |
| maximum_unused_initial_password_lifetime    | 7     |
| maximum_unused_productive_password_lifetime | 365   |
| password_expire_warning_time                | 14    |
| minimal_password_length                     | 8     |
| password_layout                             | A1a   |
| detailed_error_on_connect                   | false |
| ignore_password_exclude_list                | false |
| maximum_invalid_connect_attempts            | 6     |
| password_lock_time                          | 1440  |
| password_lock_for_system_user               | true  |
14 rows selected (overall time 1604 usec; server time 240 usec)

 Try to unlock the user DevChallenger using the user DBAdmin, like:

~/sap/hdbclient/hdbsql -U myDBAdmin -jA "ALTER USER DevChallenger RESET CONNECT ATTEMPTS"

entry.title: Re: A material is extended to new plant and storage locat...
entry.content:
Seems new condition combination like Plant/material can be added to your Freight condition type(v/07),

Also you can add customer group into this combination if planned for limited customers only & check in test system once ....

entry.title: From Holland to Harrods: Beau’s Inspiring Journey in Tech and Transportation
entry.content:
Being English and Dutch, Beau used to visit his grandparents in the Netherlands often when he was young. With his grandparents living in a farming village, it was no surprise that he developed a passion for transport, logistics and agricultural vehicles at an early stage in his life. “I dreamt of owning my own transport company with my twin brother Max, called Twin Transport, in which we would transport all types of vehicles”, the now 22-year-old explains. He has no doubts: “I think my dream and passion for supply chain and logistics really stemmed from there”.  

To ensure he continuously learns and develops his supply chain and logistics knowledge, Beau has developed a great network with senior leaders in the logistics industry at a U.K. and global level and frequently visits transport distribution centers, exhibitions, railway ports and OEMs. He also dedicated his formal education to his favorite topic, studying International Business Management with a specialization in Supply Chain and Logistics, at Amsterdam University of Applied Sciences. Being part of the SAP University Alliances program, the institution provides access to teaching and learning environments for SAP solutions, as well as digital learning resources for students and professors to upskill in the latest solutions and prepare for SAP Certification exams.  

University Lecturers Talked About High Demand for SAP Skills 

“Starting my degree in 2020 during COVID, it quickly became apparent that taking the SAP Honors Module alongside my university degree was a great opportunity to strengthen my future credentials. Back then, he says, he knew that tech and specifically SAP was in increased demand. With his lecturers making it clear, the huge demand for SAP consultants, module knowledge and certifications. “Realizing that there was a massive industry out there for me, I knew I needed to pursue SAP further in the early stages of my career”.

Following the biggest values in his life – Passion and Consistency, as well as Being Curious and Not Judgmental – Beau not only stayed on track with his studies but was also determined to obtain an SAP Certification. “To learn about SAP solutions was a real eye opener to me, identifying how many business processes can be integrated within one business solution”, he recounts. “I gained a real insight into how an SAP system can control many day-to-day business processes varying from HR, production, procurement to transportation. I knew this was the technological side of the supply chain that I needed to delve into.” 

Proud to Attach SAP Certification to CV 

As his confidence grew in the exam’s theoretical aspect, Beau was also able to apply it to practical business projects during his university modules. “So, I really had the support of both ends and realized it was a no-brainer during my degree and early stages in my career to take the SAP exam”, he explains. He graduated from university with a first-class BA honors degree and an SAP certification to go with it. “It was even better to hear from my university, that I was one of the very few to do both the practical and theoretical exam in one go, both smashing them with very high scores”, he states.  Adding, “I firmly believe taking an SAP Certification was a great decision during university, as it helped kick-start my career and made my CV very impressive at young age”.

Working for a Famous Department Store and Campaign 

Last September 2023, Beau started as a Supply Chain Graduate for the well-known luxury department store Harrods, in the United Kingdom. His first placement included being assistant Retail Manager for the Harrods Hamper department, where he successfully supported the dispatch of thousands of customer orders to the UK and internationally. In managing and overseeing a team of sales associates and operatives, ensuring the day-to-day operational retail running, and utilizing SAP solutions to make, amend, and cancel orders, Beau helped to achieve “the best record-breaking year for hampers”, he proudly states. 

Of course, Beau hasn’t forgotten about his ‘Twin Transport’ dream. “My twin brother works in the railway industry” he explains. “So, combining rail with transport and logistics gives us a massive future together: It also means we can share our stories, learn from each other and have someone along the journey to share the great success. It’s great having him with me”. 

Next up for Beau is pursuing another SAP Certification in SAP S/4HANA. “As Harrods continues to invest in SAP and S/4HANA, it will allow him to share his SAP knowledge, contribute to business projects and progress his career development at Harrods”. 

What’s in it for you? 

Did you feel inspired by Beau’s story? Then kick-start your own success story with SAP today. Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on the SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career. We are looking forward to many more great success stories! 

Find out more about how we enable the next generation to learn, research, and innovate with business applications for the Intelligent Enterprise here.

entry.title: 🎉SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement June Product Release 2024
entry.content:
SAP Emarsys' June Product Release is now LIVE and packed full of new omnichannel features and enterprise-grade enhancements to enable our customers to build, launch and scale personalized customer engagements and drive long-term customer loyalty. 

In addition, we’ve announced a suite of cutting-edge AI advancements that add important new functionality to our platform to unleash marketer creativity, improve productivity and power relevant customer experiences at scale. 

So, let’s see what’s new: 

Marketing Intelligence & AI innovations from SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement 

While AI has reemerged as a marketing sensation of late, Emarsys is not new to it. We’ve been embedding AI in our solutions for years, including: 

  • Predictive AI Segments 
  • Send Time Optimization 
  • Product Recommendations for email, web, and mobile 
  • ...just to name a few 

Building on this foundation, we’re expanding our AI capabilities to boost marketer productivity and creativity with generative AI, helping them deliver relevant experiences at scale. The new AI features we’ve introduced, include: 

  • AI Product Finder (in Pilot): to easily search within a product catalog and insert specified products and product fields 
  • AI Preheader Generator (expected GA in July): an addition to the existing Subject Line Generator toolset to generate engaging preheader copy 
  • AI Segment Generator (in Pilot): to generate human readable descriptions of existing segments for improved comprehension and searchability, plus the reverse use case in the future of generating a new segment based only on a natural language user prompt 

But, while AI is stealing the headlines, marketers still need support connecting across the experience, making data-driven decisions and adapting to shifts in the market or their business with speed and agility which is why we’re also rolling out new marketing and advertising channels, enhanced analytics, collaborative marketing tools, expanded integrations with our partner ecosystem and more in our June Product Release. Read on to learn more: 

Connected: Omnichannel Excellence 

  • Conversational Messaging: WhatsApp (Early Adopter) 
  • Digital Ads: LinkedIn Integration 
  • Web & Mobile Updates 
  • Reusable Content Blocks (in VCE) (Pilot) 
  • Omnichannel Code Editor 

Insightful: Marketer Intelligence 

  • AI Product Finder (Pilot) 
  • AI Subject Line Generator (Pilot) 
  • Campaign Analytics Enhancements 
  • Audience Reporting Enhancements 
  • Unified Dashboard (Pilot)  

Adaptive: Extensible, Composable Platform 

  • Contact Data in Open Data (Pilot) 
  • SAP CDP data in Emarsys RDS 
  • Omnichannel Voucher Management API  
  • Real-time SAP CDP Connector Enhancements 
  • mParticle Connector (Pilot) 
  • Google Analytics Integration Update 
  • Account Engagement Improvements 

Trusted: Enterprise-Grade & Cloud Scale 

  • Cloud-native Batch Sales Data Onboarding 
  • Cloud-native Batch Personalization  
  • Distribution for Omnichannel Content Blocks 
  • Bulk distribution for Automation Programs 
  • Bulk Deletion for Automation Programs 
  • Asset Tagging (Pilot) 
  • Role Assignment Enhancements 
  • Self-serve Account Owner Promotion 

That concludes all the main features released this June, if you'd like to learn more about how they all work then check out our product release blog and/or join us for our Q2 2024 Emarsys Release Recap on June 20th. And if you are interested in joining one of the active pilots or early adopter programs, please submit your request or reach out to your customer success manager.

entry.title: Pricing in Consignment Purchasing
entry.content:
Hello

My question is about pricing in consignment purchasing process. As such the configuration of this process seems in sap, there is no pricing in consignment purchase orders as there is for normal purchase orders and the pricing of consignment stock usages derives its pricing from info records and it is triggered as such stock is used via material documents.

My Issue is:

 Is there any way to enter freight costs for consignment POs and create invoices for freight costs after GR has been made for the PO? It may happen that transportation occurs for any PO including a consignment PO; so may raise the need for having freight condition records in such POs and subsequently create invoice for  such costs.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi community member, 

I am excited to join the group and for my future participation in it.

entry.title: Empty destination logistics area field in inbound delivery in SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
Hi, my customer requirement is that when logistic user creates an inbound delivery document from purchase order than the system should not propose any destination logistic area. The field should be empty. It is not possibble to create any acceptable and working rule for the destination determination.

What do you think they can achieve this goal with any configuration in byd?

Thanks in advance, Eszter

entry.content:
Fakturaposition IRIN nicht in COPA gebucht

entry.title: Error in Object Dependency Procedure
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created a procedure using CU01 and am attempting to clear a characteristic value of the 'Date Format' data type.The following is the OD code, which is currently producing a syntax error. The error disappears if I remove t line# 2. Could you please help me resolve this issue?

 

 

 $SELF.DELTA_FLAG ?= 'N' IF $SELF.REF_DELTA_TEMP EQ ' ',
 $SELF.CHANGE_EFFECTIVE_START_DATE ?= ' 'IF $SELF.REF_DELTA_TEMP EQ ' ',
 $SELF.REF_DELTA_TEMP = 'X' IF $SELF.REF_DELTA_TEMP EQ ' '.

 

 

Regards,

Mukhtar

SAP Variant Configuration and PricingPLM Variant Configuration

we have followed all the steps mentioned above and added Groups as well in our IAS tenant.

what may be the reason ?

 

"xs.saml.groups": [
"sac"
],
"xs.rolecollections": [
"sac.users"
]

 

 

Thank you,

Himanshu

I need a report which shows those columns, unfortunately could not create one myself and I feel like I am lost. Can someone please guide me?

The data I want to see:

BP Number

BP Name

Comm. method

E-mail address

Regards,

Bilge

entry.title: SAP NPV for migrated leases
entry.content:
Hi All,

Has any one implemented note 3136768 - Enhancement (BAdI): Exclusive calculation start for initial valuation,

can you please share more details about it, does it allow to have a user specified NPV value at the lease start date in SAP i.e the migration date.

Regard,

Charmaine

entry.title: Re: SBO Mail not working in New installation 10.0 FP2402
entry.content:
Hi gonzalogomez

Please help to perform the below tsk and see if this helps:

1. Please deactivate all the databases from the Alert service. You can try disable the "Job Service Alert" from General Settings for all the databases.

2. Please try check each database one by one and see if you are able to get the test alert for any user?   

3. Kindly also make sure that if you have many active alerts in any other database, please keep this test with simple database first to be sure if the issue is with Database specific or with Job Service itself. 

4. From the SLD login window, please select the instance and all the databases, click the Refresh the Database User and see if this makes any difference?

5. If possible, please try create a latest backup of any database, restore it with different name (like a test database) and check the alert only for this test database and see if this works for you?

6. Please check the same after restart the Servertools and Service Layer services to see if this works now?

7. Kindly make sure that the SAP Business One Services are running the Admin / root user privileges. 

8. If nothing helped, kindly collect all the mentioned logs as per this SAP Note "2419141" and share the correct date and time from your test.  Please create incident using component SBO-BC-JOB and attach all information collected in this step 8.

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh

entry.title: Re: Regarding FBL1N transaction report
entry.content:
Hi, Priya, we have a similar requirement as above could you please provide the code which you have.

 

Thanks 

vijay

entry.title: Re: SQL Database to datasphere vis DP connetor
entry.content: Hello @XaviPolo,

entry.title: Re: SQL Database to datasphere vis DP connetor
entry.content: The SQl database is on-prem. Yes I have erver/port . You mean to say is I need to install SAP Cloud Connector on our server or SQL or anything else ?

entry.title: Re: Object Detection with YOLOv8 and SAP Digital Manufacturing (Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi @RobertRiedel,

Thank you so much for the post it was really helpful. 


Regards,
Sayuj.

entry.title: Re: SQL Database to datasphere vis DP connetor
entry.content: Yes to allow DSP (cloud) to access your SQL Server (on-prem) you need a "tunnel" to allow connection between both worlds. So you need SCC installed in a local server. It can be on the SQL Server, although I prefer to have it on another intermediate server, that is close enough to the SQL Server so that it does not add latency to the communication.

entry.title: SAP B1 Continuous Stock with Germany Localization
entry.content:
Hello experts,

we are an international group that is setting up a new company in Germany and we need to start a new DB in SAP as a test company for the German company.

While we are waiting for the meeting with the tax consultant, we would like to start the new DB and I'm asking the community people here located in Germany what Inventory Management system you use in SAP B1 with German localization. In Germany do you typically use Continuous Stock (perpetual inventory with accounting postings) or you use periodic inventory? And what Evaluation method do you typically use in Germany? Standard, Average or FIFO? Is the Standard method even allowed in Germany?

Thank you guys!

entry.title: Is SAP Leave Management still available in S/4?
entry.content: We are on our journey to move to S/4 from ECC EHP8.  Is the SAP Leave Management functionality still available in S/4?

entry.title: Re: XSA ODATA V4 POST Error - {code: "400" Deserialization Error: Unexpected end of JSON i
entry.content: This was my issue, thank you for sharing.

entry.title: Re: XSA ODATA V4 POST Error - {code: "400" Deserialization Error: Unexpected end of JSON i
entry.content: This was my issue, thank you for sharing. I fixed it by adding a specific path to my app.use(express.json()), like so: app.use('/path', express.json()).

entry.title: Service Contract invoicing incorrect
entry.content:
Hi,
I invoiced a service contract via VF01, then noticed it was wrong and made a credit for it.

There were two invoice lines which needs to be removed, and those were set to 0 in VA42. After this tried to invoice, but it comes as incorrect status and can't make the invoice. 

Do you know how this should be handled? 

Thanks,

Jenny

entry.title: Re: Pin to Home option not available in SAC ODE mode
entry.content:
Just to confirm. Are you looking for a workaround for "Pin to Home" or one of the other limitations. There is definitely no workaround for "Pin to Home" in ODE at this time.

 

We will publish more information on watchlist as we get closer to the date where it will be released and we will at some point be seeking customer feedback on the topic so please stay tuned.

entry.title: Re: stop return s.o to be created when returned quantity...
entry.content: In standard, system will trigger a warning message and user can very well stop further processing or proceed with creating a return sale order over and above the billed quantity.  In case, you want to restrict this, go to OVAH, select message class V4 no 229 and change from W to E. Now create a return sale order over and above the original billed quantity and update here

entry.title: Re: Demystifying Document Management Service on BTP
entry.content:
Hello, thanks for this informative blog post... I have some very basic questions though, I can't seem to wrap my head around ...

Your first point is 1. Connect BTP DMS with S/4HANA on-premise:

But I have a bit different use case ... We developed a python application that connects to on-prem HANA DB. This python app then generates some PDFs and we need to upload them somewhere.

So i suppose, i don't have to connect DMS TO S/4HANA directly?

 

We have Object Store up and running with S3 , can this be used as repository?

What even is repository in this context?

Many thanks!

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Great to have you here @Marcus_Marx_Thomsen - be sure to take part in some discussions in our groups, or start one of your own!

entry.title: Condition record and "Access to condition" is set, but output type is not getting triggered auto
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm facing an issue with the output type of the billing document. I have maintained the required configuration for the output type and the condition record. When I create the billing document for the same sales org/billing type, the output is not getting triggered.

Thanks in advance!

Regards,

Divya

entry.title: Re: 999 – The Number of the BSEG
entry.content: After experience in real implementations in Hana and with open items, It will be better extend 6 digits BSEG-BUZEI. The theory is the theory. For example update douments with more 999 items in big companies.

entry.title: Re: Modeling an advanced Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @rohanc38,

Do you mean that you want to prompt an SAC user for choosing the hierarchy, when opening the report? This is not possible at time of writing nor is there such feature on the road map at time of writing. Users can choose their hierarchies in SAC using the default hierarchy selection from the dimensions.

entry.title: BTP CI-Filter condtion set up on oDATA API ( API_SALES_ORDER_SRV) using Date values
entry.content:
Hello Community,

Good day.
I need to  set up a filter condition on oData API (API_SALES_ORDER_SRV)

(CreatedByUser eq 'BTPDEVUSER' and (CreationDate ge datetimeoffset'${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd'T'00:00:00.0000000'Z'}- ${property.p_daysInPast}' and CreationDate le datetimeoffset'${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd'T'00:00:00.0000000'Z'}'))

however this filter condition could not help me to get the records at all.
Could you please provide some hints, how this filter condition would be set up

Thanks & Regards,
Sateesh N

entry.title: BTP CI-Filter condtion set up on oDATA API ( API_SALES_ORDER_SRV) using Date values
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I need to  set up a filter condition on oData API (API_SALES_ORDER_SRV)

(CreatedByUser eq 'BTPDEVUSER' and (CreationDate ge datetimeoffset'${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd'T'00:00:00.0000000'Z'}- ${property.p_daysInPast}' and CreationDate le datetimeoffset'${date:now:yyyy-MM-dd'T'00:00:00.0000000'Z'}'))

however this filter condition could not help me to get the records at all.
Could you please provide some hints, how this filter condition would be set up

Thanks & Regards,
Sateesh N

entry.title: ADT : Error trying to open object of ATC remote check
entry.content:
Hi experts

Context:
We have configured the Fiori application Abap Custom Code Migration on a Hana system SXX, and successfully executed a Central check on the ECC referenced system DYY.

After configuring ADT, I'm able to see the results of the ATC check in the ATC Result Browser tab, in section "Results of Central Checks Runs.

But once in the details section, I'm facing the following issues: 

1/ Trying to open the development objects

I have the error:

2/ Trying the recheck

I have the error

 

3/ No option to open the check in ATC Problems view. 

I have no dumps in ST22, nor explicit messages in SM21.

Many thanks for your help
Regads
Pierre

entry.title: Re: Pin to Home option not available in SAC ODE mode
entry.content: Hi @richard_mooney, will wait for your update on the Watchlist option to be release

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - script for charts does not work...
Difference between Gl balances and asset history AR02
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

While extracting FAR register from AR02 & than comparing its depreciation for the year balance with GL`s

6 Million difference is coming. While thoroughly checking just discovered that some asset`s depreciation balance is occurring more in FAR and less in GL ? Please suggest the way forward.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Hide " Create Goal" button in Latest Goal M...
entry.content:
I believe you are trying to achieve the ask as per below idea. SAP have accepted the request for development.

Next step: The request is planned for development* and will be delivered* in a future product version.

 

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence (sap.com)

entry.title: Can you please share any available documents/blog posts on the SAP recommended connectivity between
entry.content:
Hello SAP RISE Connectivity Experts,

Can you please share any available documents / blog posts on the recommended connectivity between S/4HANA hosted in RISE on GCP and existing SAP applications (eg: BW, BPC) hosted in customer's own Azure subscription?

This is required to support a business over the interim period of converting SAP ERP to S/4HANA on RISE Private Cloud.

Background/overview as follows:

  1. Existing systems (ERP, BW, BPC, few others) are hosted on customer's own Azure subscription.
  2. Customer wants to convert ERP to RISE with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPx on GCP.
  3. Post-conversion, during an interim period, they are looking to work with S/4HANA on RISE GCP with BW and BPC on Azure.
  4. As a long term plan, they would move to a combination of latest available data components either on RISE or on SAP BTP or SaaS (eg: Datasphere, SAC, BW/4HANA on RISE).

I am trying to find the optimum network / connectivity solution for the interim phase (steps 2 and 3 above).

Thanks in advance and best regards,

Shaji

entry.title: Re: Story Reports - Successfactors
entry.content: I guess there is a limit of 30 reports which can be imported. Last year I was trying same and it failed when the number of story reports was more than 30.

entry.title: Re: What’s New for SAP Build Apps – Mobile Authentication
entry.content: @Isyraf_Afifi Hi, thanks for reaching out. This feature is specific to the enterprise version of Build Apps, which runs on top of SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP). With the authentication feature, mobile apps can be more securely integrated with BTP resources on the backend, and the app's end users will be prompted to sign in so they can access it.

entry.title: Re: How To Upload Data to a Public or Private Planning Version Using a File Upload Custom Widget Wi
entry.content: Is there any option to allow upload data to a hierarchy node?

entry.title: Re: Working with SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Add-on, for Utilities
entry.content:
Hi Ankita,

at this point we do not have the support for alerts in SAP Service Cloud Version 2 - this is something we have on the roadmap for 2025.

Best  Regards,

Steen

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano  ,

I'm a recent Finance graduate from a small seaside town in Vietnam. As the first in my family to attend university, I'm proud of my academic achievements and eager to continue learning and growing professionally.

I've always been fascinated by the power of technology to transform businesses, and my interest in SAP grew as I began my career path in an SAP ERP Project. I believe that becoming an SAP consultant would allow me to combine my financial knowledge with my passion for technology, ultimately helping businesses streamline their operations and achieve their goals.

Earning an SAP certification is a crucial step in my career path, and I'm incredibly grateful for this initiative and the opportunity to potentially achieve this goal. I'm confident that the skills and knowledge I'll gain from this program will be invaluable as I embark on my journey as an SAP consultant.

I'm excited to learn from experienced mentors, connect with fellow learners, and contribute to this vibrant community.

Thank you for considering my application!

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Onboarding and Activating the Partner Cloud Test, Demo, and Development fo
entry.content: Nice blog post, thanks for sharing.

entry.title: Costing sheet in networks and network activities
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I could use your help with a topic.

I have a project strucuture composed by project definition, WBS, networks and network activities. When I create a project by copy of the standard structure, the correct costing sheet is copied. However, the costing sheet that is populated by default on the networks (standard costing sheet for production orders) is not correct and nor is the one populated in the network activities (Z costing sheet for production orders). Can anyone tell me where this is defined in the customizing?

I searched everywhere but could not find it... I'm not sure if this might not be related to the order type and someone must have added it to PP customizing since the costing sheets appearing are production ones.

Thanks in advance, Mari.

entry.title: Re: BTP CI-Filter condtion set up on oDATA API ( API_SALES_ORDER_SRV) using Date values
entry.content: It looks like you are trying to retrieve all orders created by BTPDEVUSER in which case the easiest filter to use would be CreatedByUser eq 'BTPDEVUSER'

entry.content: Very detailles step by step tutorial ! Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 1 - Setup
entry.content: Here is my submission for this week.

entry.title: HANA Design-Time HDI composite role
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to create a design-time HDI composite role using roles created in other HDI schemas. I use HANA XSA with SP7 and have containers A and B with schema roles, then I create container C with a composite role.

  1. Add services in mta for A and B
  2. Grant the roles of A and B via .hdbgrants to #OO of container C
  3. add UPS service in mta and grant system privilege ROLE ADMIN via .hdbgrants to #OO of container C
  4. Create composite role .hdbrole using schema_roles with the same roles of A and B. I can see and select the roles of A and B in the Role Editor. 

The build of .hdbrole fails with "not authorized to access the referenced object" without a guid. I suspect because of absence "grantable to other" / with_admin_option for the roles in A, B in #OO of C.

#DI.GRANT_CONTAINER_SCHEMA_ROLES doesn't have a form with _WITH_GRANT_OPTION.

I don't want to use CATALOG roles because they are not transportable.

Does anyone know how to create a composite role right in XSA or HANA Cloud?

entry.title: Re: Code Descriptions in Reports out of EC
entry.content: In story report you have the option to report on code or description or both. Try story reporting and select the fields (like company,business unit etc) and you will see the option to select code or description accordingly.

entry.title: Free text not allowed in OPSN
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I'm trying to create a substituion rule in OPSN, however, in the prerequisites window I'm not able to type anything and the screen appears greyed out. I already tried to change to expert mode and it still doesn't work... can anyone help me with this?

Thanks in advance, Mari.

entry.title: Re: BTP CI-Filter condtion set up on oDATA API ( API_SALE...
entry.content:
Hello Ryan,

Thank you. yes, it works with CreatedByUser eq 'BTPDEVUSER

However, I must use date filter in order to retreive particular date period.

 

Thank you
Sateesh Nagabhiru

entry.title: Re: Embedded SAC in Intelligent Sales&Service Cloud V2: i...
entry.content:
Hi @KatarzynaS ,

This is currently not possible. SAP advised us, if you want to combine data from different sources to use a separate/standalone SAC/BW system. To extract your data to such a system from v2 should be able at the end of this year.

Simon

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration: Understanding “Simple Signer” [5] : Security Enhancement
entry.content: How can I create a signature using RSASSA-PSS signature algorithm?

entry.title: Re: BTP CI-Filter condtion set up on oDATA API ( API_SALES_ORDER_SRV) using Date values
entry.content: Hello Ryan, Thank you. yes, it works with CreatedByUser eq 'BTPDEVUSER However, I must use date filter in order to retreive particular date period.

entry.title: Re: C4C Service Ticket VS S4 Hana Service order vs S4 Han...
entry.content: Solution option A: Legacy system X -> s4 Hana Service order (SRVO) -> RE2 (Advance returns) if exchange required ->Warranty claim document in S4 Hana (IC &OC Versions)-> PO/Credit memo

entry.title: Identifying corresponding MRP Items give an order
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I need assistance with linking SAP tables for my stamping operation.
Specifically, I want to achieve the following:

For a given planned order (from the PLAF table) or a production order (from the AFKO/AFPO tables),
I want to retrieve all corresponding primary and secondary demands.

  • If an order is a primary, I want to get all MRP-generated items corresponding to this primary demand.
  • If an order is a secondary (SekBed), I want to identify its corresponding primary demand.

I would be thankful for any advice!

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration: Understanding “Simple Signer” [5] : Security Enhancement
entry.content:
Can I use "forge" in iFlow? I would like to migrate the below code, which is used in the PostMan environment, to build the signature header.

function loadPrivateKey(privateKeyPem, privateKeyPassphrase) {
return forge.pki.decryptRsaPrivateKey(privateKeyPem, privateKeyPassphrase);
}


function buildEncodedSignature(privateKey, signingString) {
let messageDigest = forge.md.sha256.create();
messageDigest.update(signingString, "utf8");
let pss = forge.pss.create({
md: forge.md.sha256.create(),
mgf: forge.mgf.mgf1.create(forge.md.sha256.create()),
saltLength: 32
});
let signature = privateKey.sign(messageDigest, pss);
return base64UrlEncode(signature)
}

entry.title: Re: BTP CI-Filter condtion set up on oDATA API ( API_SALE...
entry.content:
The reason you are not getting results is because your query filter is incorrectly formatted. A datetimeoffset does not include math regarding number of days, but rather a difference between UTC... e.g. eastern time zone is -05:00. Based on your expression it would be easier if you were to use datetime and reference KBA 2933028 for the proper syntax.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA – Upgrade Faster – Resolving CDS view lifecycle impacts
entry.content: Rahul- what a well written and valuable summary of managing CDS views across S4 hana upgrade and data model related changes.

entry.title: Useful SP_TransactionNotification ChapterID Mandatory Item master Data Item Master SAP Business One
entry.content:
SAP b1 SP_TransactionNotification ChapterID Mandatory Item master Data Item Master

IF @transaction_type IN ('A', 'U') AND @object_type = '4'

BEGIN

If (SELECT T0.ChapterID FROM OITM T0 Inner Join OCHP t1 on T1.AbsEntry=T0.ChapterID where T0.ItemCode=@list_of_cols_val_tab_del) IS NULL

begin

SET @error = 10

SET @error_message = N'Please Add ChapterID In Item Master'

end

END

entry.title: Re: AT END of in Custom CDS S4HANA CLOUD
entry.content:
Hello, 

Yes, we can use the AT END OF functionality in a custom CDS view to sum up the results of columns based on keys defined in the CDS.

Take an example of a custom CDS view called ZCDS_SalesData, which has the following key fields: ProductID, SalesDate, and SalesAmount. If we want to create a calculated column that sums up the SalesAmount for each ProductID and SalesDate combination. We can achieve this by using the AT END OF functionality in the CDS view.

Here's an example of how we can do this:

"sql
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCDS_SalesData'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Sales Data'
define view ZCDS_SalesData
as select from SalesData
{
key ProductID,
key SalesDate,
sum( SalesAmount ) as TotalSalesAmount
}
where ProductID = @ObjectKey.ProductID
group by ProductID, SalesDate
with
{
AT END OF ProductID,
key SalesDate,
sum( TotalSalesAmount ) as GrandTotalSalesAmount
}
"

In this example, we are creating a calculated column called TotalSalesAmount using the sum() function to sum up the SalesAmount for each ProductID and SalesDate combination. We then use the AT END OF functionality to create another calculated column called GrandTotalSalesAmount, which sums up the TotalSalesAmount for each ProductID.

This allows you to group and summarize the sales data based on the keys defined in the CDS view, and calculate the total sales amount for each combination of ProductID and SalesDate.

 

Thank you.

entry.title: About MF60 use?
entry.content: It has 2 warehouses. 1. Material warehouse --> The warehouse location where stocks are entered and stored. 2-Production warehouse --> Materials needed during production are transferred to this warehouse and planned orders are created in this warehouse location. Before production, we check the material readiness using the MF60 screen. This screen shows only the stocks in the production warehouse as available stock and gives a quantity deficit. In fact, it should show the quantity deficit using the total stock in production and material warehouse. Is there a method for this?

entry.title: Different number ranges for networks
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I would like to have different number ranges for standard networks and "regular" networks.

I had a client that had the external number range for standard networks and the internal number range for the "regular" networks but I can't seem to fins where I can define this. Can anyone help me?

Thanks in advance, Mari.

entry.title: Re: ADD NEW ROW IN (REQUEST FOR QUOTATION)RFQ SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi eonsase 

Regarding the RFQ customization , you can find documentation under B1if -> Scenarios -> Setup -> select "sap.B1RFQ" for scenario Package Identifier field -> Then click "Documentation" Button -> search for Section 3.3 "Adding a Custom  to the RFQ Template"

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support
